title: Software Engineer - AI Commerce
company_focus:
Building a truth engine for shopping - verified product
research
description:
Doing product research online feels harder than ever. AI
promised better information. Instead it flooded the web with
hallucinations, generated content, and confident-sounding
answers that have no idea what's true.

Product.ai is building the truth engine for shopping. When
every other AI tool summarizes and guesses, we verify. Not
opinions. Not averages. Ground truth.

Self-funded and profitable for over a decade, we're a team
of e-commerce veterans who have been building commerce truth
systems at scale. Over a billion dollars in annual
transaction volume. Millions of customers served. Product.ai
is our flagship - the new starting point for shopping in the
AI era. We're in early alpha, building alongside our
community of domain experts.

We are seeking a Software Engineer to build and ship our
consumer-facing product surfaces. This is not a feature
factory role. You own the full stack from concept through
live user feedback, you trust AI to multiply your output,
and you measure yourself on user impact, not pull request
counts. Our engineering team builds at a pace that
competitors with 10x our headcount can't match. We need
someone who makes that ratio even more lopsided.

Based in Santa Monica. Hybrid schedule with flexibility. For
the right builder, we're open to remote.

What You Will Build

- The Velocity Loop. You use AI to write code, tests, and
  documentation. LLMs are core infrastructure in your
  workflow, not toys. You know what to automate
  (boilerplate, test scaffolding, documentation) and what to
  think about (architecture, edge cases, user behavior). AI-
  augmented development is how we maintain a 20-person team
  that outships companies with 200.
- Full-Stack Feature Delivery. You own features from React
  frontend through Node/Python backend to PostgreSQL schema.
  You design the data model, build the API, ship the
  interface, and measure whether it worked. One engineer,
  full loop.
- Product-Minded Engineering. You're solving user problems,
  not executing specifications. You push back on
  requirements that don't make sense. You suggest
  alternatives that ship in two days instead of two weeks.
  When the data says users don't care about the feature you
  just built, you kill it and move on.
- Autonomous Quality Ownership. You own quality end-to-end.
  You write tests, you deploy with confidence, you built the
  monitoring that tells you when something breaks. There is
  no QA team. There is no separate ops team. You build it,
  you ship it, you own it.
- Foundational contributions. Every engineer's code becomes
  infrastructure someone else builds on. You choose boring
  technology when boring technology works. You introduce
  complexity only when the problem demands it.

Who You Are

- You ship fast and you ship often. You'd rather have a good
  enough solution in production generating real user
  feedback than a perfect solution sitting in code review.
  Your instinct when faced with ambiguity is to build
  something small and learn, not to schedule a meeting and
  discuss.
- You're a product builder, not a coder. You care about user
  problems, not clever code. When you look at a ticket, you
  ask "what user behavior are we trying to change?" before
  you ask "what's the technical approach?" You've killed
  your own features when the data showed they weren't
  working.
- You use AI as a force multiplier. You write code with AI
  assistance daily. You're clear-eyed about what it's good
  for and where it fails. You are not ideological about AI.
  You are practical. You have measurably increased your
  shipping velocity using AI tools.
- You prefer building to process. You'd rather ship a feature
  than attend a planning meeting. You've worked in
  environments with heavy process and light process, and you
  strongly prefer light. You can self-organize without a
  project manager.
- You take initiative and own outcomes. You see a problem, you
  propose a solution, you ship it. When something you built
  breaks, you fix it - you don't file a ticket.

Ownership

We operate as a high-performance studio, not a typical
corporation. Every builder at Product.ai is a partner, not
an employee collecting a paycheck.

- Base: $280,000 - $350,000. Top of market. Your base covers
  your life; your ownership builds your freedom.
- Equity: You receive a formal ownership stake via Profits
  Interest Units (PIUs) - Class B Membership Interests with
  a $0 strike price. Not options. Not RSUs. Actual ownership
  that participates in the upside from day one and qualifies
  for Capital Gains tax treatment.
- Profit Sharing: You participate in the company's annual
  success. As an equity holder, you receive a pro-rata share
  of Free Cash Flow (FCF) - real cash, every year, not a
  promise tied to a future exit.
- Liquidity: We operate an Annual Tender Offer where the
  company buys back vested interests at fair market value.
  You can turn ownership into cash every year. No waiting
  for an IPO. No hoping for an acquisition.
- Benefits: 100% premium coverage for you and your family,
  daily catered lunches, and unlimited PTO that we actually
  expect you to use.

How to Apply

We skip the 60-minute recruiter screen. Instead, you'll
answer a few short video questions so we can get to know how
you think and work. Less time than a phone screen, and you
can do it whenever works for you.

We're not looking for polished presentations. We're looking
for signal on who you are.
responsibilities: Build and ship consumer-facing product surfaces end-to-end from React frontend through Node/Python backend to PostgreSQL
Use AI-assisted development workflows to write code, tests, and documentation at high velocity
Own full-stack feature delivery including data model design, API development, UI implementation, and impact measurement
Practice product-minded engineering by solving user problems, pushing back on weak requirements, and iterating based on data
Autonomously own quality end-to-end including testing, deployment, and monitoring with no separate QA or ops teams
Make foundational contributions to shared codebase infrastructure using appropriate technology choices
use_cases: AI-powered product research and truth verification for shopping
Consumer-facing commerce surfaces that provide verified product information
Ground truth product data systems at scale serving millions of customers
ai_classification_reasoning:
This is a full-stack software engineering role at an AI
company where the engineer builds consumer-facing product
surfaces (React frontend, Node/Python backend, PostgreSQL).
AI/LLMs are used as development tools to multiply output,
not as the thing being built. The engineer is not building,
training, fine-tuning, or deploying AI models — they're
building web products powered by AI that others have
created.
